heading: Officers and enlisted members of the Navy Reserve and Marine Corps Reserve: 30 years; 20 years; retired pay
label: 8327 Officers and enlisted members of the Navy Reserve and Marine Corps Reserve: 30 years; 20 years; retired pay
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)6327, (b), (d), (e), (f).50 U.S.C. 1052(a), (b), (d), (e), (f), .July 9, 1952, ch. 608, § 413(a)66 Stat. 499In subsection (a) the word “Federal” is omitted and the words “in the armed forces, other than active duty for training” are inserted. The words “active Federal service” are not defined in . Section 310 of the Naval Reserve Act of 1938, which replaced, specifies active service in the “Army, Navy, Marine Corps, Coast Guard, Naval Auxiliary Service, Naval Reserve Force, Naval Militia in Federal status, National Naval Volunteers, Naval Reserve, Marine Corps Reserve Force, and Marine Corps Reserve.” was intended to preserve the rights of persons who, on , were members of reserve components, so that they would not be prejudiced by the repeal of § 310 of the Naval Reserve Act of 1938 (U.S. Code Congressional and Administrative News, 1952, p. 3584). To effect that purpose, the service that was creditable under the 1938 Act must be creditable under . The words “active service in the armed forces, other than active duty for training” cover all creditable service. The Judge Advocate General of the Navy, in an opinion dated (JAG II:2:WGA:CA:mk), held that active duty for training was not creditable under the 1938 Act and is, therefore, not creditable under the 1952 Act.50 U.S.C. 105250 U.S.C. 105250 U.S.C. 105250 U.S.C. 1052January 1, 1953August 27, 1954

Amendments2018—, renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232, § 807(b)(15)section 6327 of this titleSubsec. (e). , substituted “section 8330” for “section 6330”.Pub. L. 115–232, § 809(a)2006—, substituted “Navy Reserve” for “Naval Reserve” in section catchline.Pub. L. 109–163, § 515(b)(3)(B)Subsecs. (a), (c). , substituted “Navy Reserve” for “Naval Reserve”.Pub. L. 109–163, § 515(b)(1)(I)1963—Subsec. (b). substituted “of the grade in which retired” for “to which he would be entitled if on active duty” after “50 percent of the basic pay”.Pub. L. 88–1321958—Subsec. (e). entitled eligible members of Naval Reserve or Marine Corps Reserve to elect to transfer to Fleet Reserve or Fleet Marine Corps Reserve.Pub. L. 85–583

children.text: at least 30 years of active service in the armed forces, other than active duty for training; or

at least 20 years of active service in the armed forces other than active duty for training, the last 10 of which he served in the 11-year period immediately preceding his transfer to the Retired Reserve.

children.label: b
children.text: Each member who is transferred to the Retired Reserve under subsection (a) is entitled, when not on active duty, to retired pay at the rate of 50 percent of the basic pay of the grade in which retired.

children.label: c
children.text: This section applies only to persons who were members of the Navy Reserve or the Marine Corps Reserve on .January 1, 1953

children.label: d
children.text: This section terminates on . However, its termination will not affect any accrued rights to retired pay.January 1, 1973

children.label: e
children.text: A member who is eligible for retirement under this section, and who is also eligible for retirement under another provision or for transfer to the Fleet Reserve or the Fleet Marine Corps Reserve under , is entitled to elect which of these benefits he is to receive.section 8330 of this title
